summary: Small single-storey rectangular building with a raised brick-filled flat central roof, a stone-slab coping, smooth light outer trim and a lower band of pale textured blocks for walls; continuous horizontal window strip on long sides and a low surrounding parapet/paved border at ground level.

shape.overall_form: rectangular prism with flat recessed roof and a surrounding low border
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (mirrored windows and trim)

elements: continuous horizontal window strip on both long walls (series of 1x1 glass panes set in row)
single-storey walls with corner trim columns
brick-filled recessed flat roof framed by stone slabs
low raised perimeter border/pavement around building (one block high)
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular base roughly 9 x 17 blocks (estimated) elevated on a 1-block-high surrounding quartz border.

Walls: a lower ~2-block high band of pale textured blocks (sandstone) topped by a 1-block high smooth light trim (quartz) containing a continuous row of 1-block-high windows across the long sides; corners use the same smooth trim blocks as vertical corner columns.
Above trim place a 1-block-high layer of brick as a parapet, then frame a recessed flat roof area with stone-slab/stonebrick slabs around the top edge and fill the recessed center with bricks.
Windows are set as a horizontal strip: repeated glass panes with single-block spacing matching photos (no visible shutters/doors on shown sides).
Surround immediate base with a 1-block-high pathway border (quartz) set inside a shallow excavated dirt moat visible around the plot.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone/quartz/stone slab are inferred by color/texture and may represent other smooth/pale blocks (e.g., smooth sandstone, smooth quartz, diorite); confidence given per material.
Precise footprint and internal layout: top-down images suggest rectangular roof 9x17 but exact block counts could differ by +/-1-2 blocks.
Entrance location is not clearly visible in provided views; a doorway may exist on an unseen short side or be on the visible side but obscured.
Roof center material looks like brick tiles but could be a different red block variant; coping slabs might be stonebrick or smooth stone slabs.
